chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1500 of 2021 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection For hearing of main case 03.11.2021 Mr. Rizwan Ahmed Memon advocate for petitioners along with petitioners. M/s Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Addl. A.G. and Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Addl. P.G. along with Inspector Saifullah Bughio SHO P.S A-Section Nawabshah, ASI Sanjar Khan Jamali on behalf of SHO P.S B-Section Nawabshah and ASI Muhammad Parial on behalf of DSP Kazi Ahmed. -.-.-. Petitioners acting as complainants registered one FIR each bearing Crime Nos. 184 and 198 of 221 at P.S A-Section Nawabshah, u/s 489-F P.P.C. against respondent No.1. Apprehending favour to him in investigation by the local police, they have filed this petition seeking direction for appointment of an impartial police officer to reinvestigate the matter and provide protection to them and their family members against highhandedness of respondent No.1. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioners. He has submitted that both the FIRs although were cancelled by the local police but their opinion was not accepted by the learned Magistrates concerned and they took cognizance of offence. Currently respondent No.1 is standing trial in both the cases. We feel in the facts and circumstances grievance of the petitioners has been redressed. Insofar as question of protection to them against alleged highhandedness of respondent No.1. Prima facie, there is no material available on record establishing that they have been subjected to any harassment by him on any occasion. In any case, it is a right of every person to be provided protection in accordance with law.

Prima facie, there is no material available on record establishing that they have been subjected to any harassment by him on any occasion. In any case, it is a right of every person to be provided protection in accordance with law. In the same vein, while disposing of this petition we direct the police concerned to provide protection to petitioners as and when required in accordance with law. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
